This will bring social security to unorganised workers, including gig and platform workers. Mandatory health check-ups and unemployment benefits will be introduced. A social security fund will be established for 400 million unorganised workers.

items.title: EPL, Indovida to merge in $2 billion packaging deal
items.summary: The transaction will create a diversified, multi-format packaging platform with a combined valuation of about $2 billion. EPL is being valued at about $1.2 billion-about 70% higher than its closing price on Friday-while Indovida India is valued at roughly $700 million.

items.title: Some PM KUSUM projects get extension till March 2027
items.summary: The government has extended the deadline for some PM KUSUM solar projects. Developers and state agencies now have until March 31, 2027, to commission projects with power purchase agreements or letters of intent signed by December 31, 2025. This move offers relief amid execution delays and financing challenges. States are coordinating with banks to facilitate project completion.

items.title: Centre looks to trigger 'Essentials' Act to regulate prices of bulk drugs
items.summary: Drug prices in India: The department of pharmaceuticals (DoP) has been holding meetings with industry executives to take stock of the situation, people in the know said.

items.title: CII calls for steady supply chains, jobs stability amid West Asis crisis
items.summary: The Confederation of Indian Industry, or CII, has proposed a 12-point plan for businesses. This agenda aims to manage challenges from the West Asia conflict. CII calls for joint efforts to secure supply chains and keep prices steady. The focus is on protecting jobs amidst global uncertainty. Industry must also build strategic reserves and pass on savings to consumers.

items.title: Gulf crisis drags down gold demand
items.summary: Chains such as Malabar Gold & Diamonds, Kalyan Jewellers, Joyalukkas and Titan Company's Tanishq, which together command a significant presence in the region, are seeing weak footfall and cautious spending, even as most stores remain open in markets such as the UAE.

items.title: Airlines brace for a hard landing as ATF prices surge
items.summary: Prepare for potentially higher airfare this summer! With escalating jet fuel prices and a lack of available pilots, airlines are bracing for challenging times. IndiGo is scaling back its flight offerings, and Air India alongside Air India Express is eyeing cautious expansion.

items.title: SFIO summons former top executives of IndusInd Bank in accounting probe
items.summary: The Serious Fraud Investigation Office is investigating IndusInd Bank's accounting irregularities. Key former executives have been summoned for questioning. This probe follows earlier findings by auditors and forensic reports. Meanwhile, Mumbai Police's Economic Offences Wing is nearing the closure of its preliminary inquiry, finding no evidence of fund diversion.

items.title: SFIO summons former top executives of IndusInd Bank in accounting probe
items.summary: The Serious Fraud Investigation Office is investigating IndusInd Bank's accounting irregularities. Key former executives have been summoned for questioning. This probe follows earlier findings by auditors and forensic reports. Meanwhile, Mumbai Police's Economic Offences Wing is nearing the closure of its preliminary inquiry, finding no evidence of fund diversion.

items.title: Pitch perfect for private equity as the IPL investment playbook draws global investors
items.summary: Several team owners have extended their footprint into overseas T20 leagues such as South Africa's SA20, the UAE's ILT20 and England's The Hundred. This has effectively transformed franchises into multi-league cricket portfolios, transforming them from single-league entities into scalable global platforms with diversified revenue streams and greater upside for investors.

items.title: Tech prices surge: Phones, appliances set to cost more
items.summary: Electronics costs are soaring to unprecedented heights, disrupting the long-standing trend of falling prices. Basic gadgets like televisions and smartphones are now pricier, while crucial appliances such as refrigerators and air conditioners are expected to follow suit.

items.title: RBI's currency defence could leave banks paying the price
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has introduced a bold new daily limit on the open rupee positions held by banks, aiming to counteract the rupee's weakening trend versus the dollar. As a result, banks are now required to trim their dollar assets and offload US currency.

items.title: RBI's currency defence could leave banks paying the price
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India has introduced a bold new daily limit on the open rupee positions held by banks, aiming to counteract the rupee's weakening trend versus the dollar. As a result, banks are now required to trim their dollar assets and offload US currency.

items.title: Market reset: India pulls the plug on Chinese CCTV makers
items.summary: As of April 1, new legislation will prevent the use of Chinese CCTV cameras in India, directly affecting companies such as Hikvision and Dahua. In their place, Indian manufacturers are seizing the opportunity to fill the market void, which is inevitably influencing price trends. With these regulations, the govt seeks to bolstering security protocols across the nation.

items.title: Madhya Pradesh revokes Bank of Baroda ban in less than 24 hours
items.summary: The order, issued on March 27, had barred the BoB from handling government transactions for five years, citing non-compliance with official instructions on fund transfers under the state's chief minister Kisan Yojana.

items.title: Madhya Pradesh revokes Bank of Baroda ban in less than 24 hours
items.summary: The order, issued on March 27, had barred the BoB from handling government transactions for five years, citing non-compliance with official instructions on fund transfers under the state's chief minister Kisan Yojana.

items.title: DoT rules out Vi-like relief for Airtel, Tata Teleservices; telcos must pay ₹10,000 cr AGR dues by March 31
items.summary: Bharti Airtel and Tata Teleservices face a March 31 deadline to pay approximately ₹10,000 cr in adjusted gross revenue dues. This payment is mandated by a Supreme Court order. Unlike Vodafone Idea, these companies have not received specific relief. Failure to comply will be considered contempt of court. Bharti Airtel owes over ₹5,000 cr, and Tata Teleservices over ₹4,000 cr.

items.title: Shapoorji Pallonji seeks bondholder nod for LTV relief on earlier NCDs
items.summary: In light of recent geopolitical unrest, the Shapoorji Pallonji Group is reaching out to bondholders for consent to adjust a crucial loan-to-value covenant tied to its rupee notes. This strategic decision to elevate the LTV threshold for a short span of three months seeks to provide the group with much-needed breathing room amidst current market fluctuations.

items.title: US pharma giant Eli Lilly, Insilico Medicine secure deal worth $2.75 billion to bring AI-developed drugs to market
items.summary: US pharmaceutical giant Eli Lilly has reached a deal worth $2.75 billion with Insilico Medicine. Under the agreement, Lilly will bring AI-developed drugs to the global market, while Insilico will receive $115 million upfront, with the remainder tied to regulatory approvals and commercial milestones

items.title: Govt eases kerosene rules for quick household distribution amid LPG supply crunch due to West Asia war
items.summary: The central government has relaxed petroleum safety and licensing rules for faster kerosene distribution to households due to the US-Israel-Iran war's impact on LPG supply, enabling ad-hoc distribution in 21 states and UTs.

items.title: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal to visit Canada in May for trade talks
items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal will visit Canada in May 2026. This visit coincides with ongoing talks for a free trade agreement between India and Canada. Discussions will focus on expediting these negotiations. Both nations will explore cooperation in clean energy, nuclear energy, agriculture, and critical minerals. The minister also met with UK and EU officials regarding trade pacts.

items.title: Calls for tiered charges on UPI reach Parliament as govt incentives seem insufficient
items.summary: A parliamentary committee has recommended introducing tiered charges for UPI transactions to address a significant funding gap and slowing growth. While the government maintains UPI is a ‘public good’, industry players argue that subsidies cover only a fraction of costs.

items.title: Voice of Global South: India bats strongly to shield WTO’s core mandates at MC14
items.summary: Successfully stalls China-led investment pact but under pressure on e-commerce tax moratorium

items.title: Piyush Goyal meets UK, EU, NZ, Canada trade ministers at WTO MC14 sidelines
items.summary: India is actively pursuing trade agreements with the EU and UK. Discussions are underway to finalize a free trade deal with the EU. The UK has completed its parliamentary approval for the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement. New Zealand is preparing for Prime Minister Modi's visit with key deliverables. Canada and India are expediting their trade negotiations.

items.title: Bihar planning market debut of transmission company BSPTCL: State Energy Secy
items.summary: Bihar State Power Transmission Company Ltd (BSPTCL) is set to become the first state-owned transmission utility to go public, with the listing proposed on the NSE

items.title: RBI cap to force banks unwind long dollar bets; Rupee seen surging up by 100 paise
items.summary: The directive asks Authorised Dealers to ensure that their Net Open Position – Indian Rupee (NOP-INR) in the onshore deliverable market should be maintained within US$ 100 million at the end of each business day.

items.title: Blackstone-backed EPL to merge with Indovida to make $2 billion packaging giant
items.summary: Blackstone-backed EPL will continue as the listed entity after the merger. Indorama will nominate at least three directors to its board, while Blackstone will nominate one. Since it is a share swap, there is no regulatory requirement for an open offer.

items.title: Last rites of industrialist Vijaypat Singhania performed in Mumbai
items.summary: Vijaypat Singhania headed the Raymond Group for two decades, and was also a recipient of the Padma Bhushan

items.title: Wipro bets on regional depth, convenience to scale foods business
items.summary: Wipro is expanding Brahmins into adjacent categories like millets and snacks, with 7–10 SKUs already launched, as it broadens its FMCG play

items.title: Middle East Crisis: Govt cuts duty by Rs 10, raises LPG supply to 70%; 55+ lakh domestic refills delivered in a day
items.summary: Middle East Conflict India Impact: India has assured adequate petrol and diesel availability amid the Middle East crisis, cutting excise duty by Rs 10 and boosting supply measures to prevent shortages and panic buying. Over 55 lakh domestic LPG refills were delivered in a day, with commercial LPG supply raised to 70 per cent and gas distribution prioritised for households and transport.

items.title: A.P. poised for a leap in shipbuilding, ship repair cluster
India relaxes kerosene rules to tackle energy supply issues
items.summary: India is easing petroleum rules to speed up kerosene delivery to homes. This move comes as the Iran war impacts global energy supplies. The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas announced these changes. They will allow ad-hoc kerosene distribution for cooking and lighting. This will benefit households across 21 states and federal territories.

items.summary: Under the deal, EPL is valued at around $1.2 billion, implying a price of ₹339 per share—about 70 per cent higher than its previous closing price—while Indovida is valued at roughly $0.7 billion, at a 35 per cent discount to EPL’s valuation multiple

items.title: Gold, silver to remain range-bound, Powell speech, West Asia in focus: Analysts
items.summary: Gold futures in the domestic market fell slightly to ₹1.44 lakh per 10 grams, while silver rose by ₹1,182 (0.52%) to ₹2.27 lakh per kilogram on the Multi Commodity Exchange

items.title: 6,000 PNG users surrender LPG connections after govt revises rule amid Iran war
items.summary: The government has mandated households with piped natural gas (PNG) to surrender their subsidized domestic LPG connections, with 6,000 users already complying. This move aims to prioritize LPG for those without PNG access amidst global energy supply disruptions. Officials urge more PNG consumers to voluntarily give up their LPG connections to aid those in need.

items.title: No LPG dry out, ‘sufficient’ stock of petrol, diesel, two tankers enroute to India amid West Asia war: Check top updates
items.summary: The Central government said it is ensuring stability in energy supplies amid the West Asia conflict and urged citizens to avoid panic buying, use digital modes for LPG bookings. Here are the top updates:

items.title: Airtel to pay  ₹10,000 crore AGR dues in first post-moratorium instalment
items.summary: Bharti Airtel’s deferred payment liability towards AGR was at  ₹38,604 crore, according to its FY25 annual report. With interest accrued, the same has risen over  ₹40,000 crore.

items.title: EPL, Indovida to merge in $2 billion deal to create consumer packaging unit
items.summary: EPL Limited and Indovida India Private Limited are merging. This creates a major consumer packaging company. The combined entity will be valued at approximately $2 billion. Annual revenue is expected to reach about $1 billion. Indorama Ventures will be a co-promoter with a significant stake. Blackstone will also hold a stake. This merger is set to reshape the packaging industry.

items.title: Bitcoin dips after steadying over last week
items.summary: The near-term outlook remains highly sensitive to global cues, with Bitcoin continuing to track broader financial conditions and investor risk appetite

items.title: Airlines to offer 60 pc seats in flights without additional charges from Apr 20
items.summary: Airlines must now offer a minimum of 60 percent of flight seats without extra charges starting April 20. The DGCA has issued new directives for transparent seat allocation. Passengers on the same booking will be seated together. This move aims to ensure fair access for all travelers. Airlines are preparing to implement these changes.

items.title: HC suggests mediation as Kalyani siblings battle over family wealth
items.summary: Babasaheb Kalyani, Sugandha Hiremath, Gaurishankar Kalyani, and their children have been embroiled in legal battles over ancestral wealth exceeding  ₹1 trillion, including vast land holdings and promoter stakes in listed companies such as flagship Bharat Forge Ltd.

items.title: PM Modi to inaugurate Kaynes Semicon OSAT facility at Sanand on March 31
items.summary: This will be the third semiconductor-related inauguration in the fast-emerging industrial cluster near Ahmedabad in Gujarat

items.title: Real estate premiumization not a temporary trend, say experts
items.summary: The real estate sector is witnessing a permanent shift toward premiumization as buyers and companies prioritize global-standard quality over price. Driven by increased capital access and the need to retain talent, the luxury segment continues to grow even as mass-market housing demand cools.

items.title: India proposes “new approaches” for farm talks at WTO consistent with Doha mandate
items.summary: India urged the WTO to prioritize the development dimension of the Doha Work Programme, emphasizing mandated issues like Public Stockholding and Special Safeguard Mechanism. New Delhi stressed these should be addressed independently, not contingent on other reforms, and called for strengthening special and differential treatment for developing nations.

items.title: Govt may unveil more relief packages for MSMEs, vulnerable sectors if West Asia crisis worsens
items.summary: India's government is considering further economic relief for vulnerable sectors and MSMEs. This comes as the West Asia crisis continues, impacting global crude prices and supply chains. Measures like excise duty cuts on fuel and support for exporters are already in place. The government aims to shield the economy from rising costs and potential disruptions.

items.title: As Jewar gears up, Delhi airport firm seeks jet fuel tax parity to stay in the game
items.summary: GMR Airports has urged Delhi to cut VAT on jet fuel to 1–4% from 25%, warning that higher taxes could shift airline traffic to the upcoming Noida International Airport, where ATF tax is just 1%.

items.title: Iran slams US ‘15-point plan’, warns of ‘false-flag’ threat and wider war; ‘we will never be humiliated’
items.summary: Iran has rejected the US ‘15-point plan’, warning it could trigger wider conflict and alleging risks of ‘false-flag’ operations. Tehran says it will not bow to pressure, signalling rising tensions in the Middle East.

items.title: ATR to reinforce India presence, explore avenues to expand capacity
items.summary: India is already the second largest market for the European plane maker after Indonesia with around 70 ATR-72 and ATR-42 aircraft serving IndiGo, Alliance Air and FLY91

items.summary: Indian markets ended a two-day rally as Iran-US tensions and elevated energy prices weighed on investor sentiment. Nifty fell 2.09% to 22,819.60, Sensex down 2.25%. US and European indices also declined amid geopolitical uncertainty. Financials, autos, and consumer stocks led losses, while volatility remained elevated.

items.title: Indian refiners bought 60 million barrels of Russian crude oil since March 5: Report
items.summary: The Oxford Institute for Energy Studies (OIES) in its recent energy comment pointed out that the scale of the Strait of Hormuz disruption is like “no other seen in oil market history”

items.summary: Celebrity weddings are now high-value marketing ecosystems, where brand deals, influencer buzz and timed campaigns turn personal milestones into sustained commercial opportunity.&nbsp;

items.title: Govt shares key updates on oil, gas shortage, shipping & more amid Israel-Iran war
items.summary: India is actively managing its oil and gas supplies amidst global conflicts. Key LPG carriers are en route, and the nation is prioritizing natural gas for essential consumers. The government is also expediting infrastructure development and advising consumers on alternative fuels. Efforts are underway to ensure the safe return of Indian citizens from affected regions.

items.title: Civil aviation ministry looks at multiple options to minimise West Asia crisis impact on airlines
items.summary: A highly placed source said the ministry was considering multiple options as part of larger efforts to reduce the impact of the West Asia conflict on the carriers

items.summary: Digi Yatra is a Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI) based ecosystem utilising face biometric technology for contactless and seamless passenger processing at airports

items.title: Annihilation of caste: Why capitalism is more likely to do the job than redistributive ideas of communism
items.summary: Markets are indifferent to lineage. They accord status not by birth but by one’s ability to create value. This reshapes opportunities and melts barriers in ways that revolutionary policies of redistribution can’t. Let capital do the job.

items.title: Civil aviation ministry looks at multiple options to minimise West Asia crisis impact on airlines
items.summary: Airlines are grappling with soaring operational costs due to the West Asia conflict, prompting the civil aviation ministry to explore tax cuts on jet fuel with state governments.  Skyrocketing oil prices and longer flight paths are significantly impacting carriers. The DGCA is actively monitoring the situation and engaging with international counterparts.

items.title: Michael Bloomberg: The US administration’s assault on data will hurt governance, markets and the economy
items.summary: Fears have arisen over America’s statistical system losing its integrity. As politics creeps in, the risks go far beyond transparency. It threatens policymaking, market confidence and the economy. America needs reliable facts, not dodgy data.

items.title: Authorities were 'moderate' in approving summer schedule flights after IndiGo crisis in December: Source
items.summary: Airlines will operate 10% fewer flights this summer due to civil aviation authorities adopting a more cautious approach. This decision follows IndiGo's significant disruptions last December, prompting regulators to prioritize aircraft and pilot availability before approving more services. The summer schedule, running from March 29 to October 24, will see a reduction of approximately 2,561 weekly flights.

items.title: Anil Agarwal says Vedanta got written win for Jaypee Group asset bid, later reversed
items.summary: Mining baron Anil Agarwal stated Vedanta was initially declared the highest bidder for a Jaypee Group asset. However, the decision was later reversed. Gautam Adani's group was the only other bidder. The National Company Law Tribunal approved Adani's bid, which Vedanta has challenged. The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal is hearing the case.

items.title: Good news for passengers: Railways plans decongestion at 76 stations; Ashwini Vaishnaw announces tech upgrades, stricter entry rules
items.summary: Indian Railways plans to decongest 76 major stations with AI surveillance, holding areas and stricter entry rules, Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said. The move aims to reduce crowding, improve safety and streamline passenger movement across the Indian Railways network.

items.title: CaratLane eyes retail expansion with 40 new stores in FY27
items.summary: Jewellery brand CaratLane is set to expand its retail footprint by opening 40 new stores in the upcoming financial year. The company plans to own 10 percent of these new outlets. Expansion efforts will primarily target the northern, eastern, and southern regions of India.

items.title: Vedanta chief Anil Agarwal flags Jaypee asset bid reversal after written confirmation
items.summary: Vedanta would present facts through appropriate channels and follow due process, he said.

items.title: Adequate fertiliser stock available, farmers will not face shortage despite Israel-Iran war: IFFCO
items.summary: India's fertiliser supply remains steady despite the West Asia crisis. Production and distribution are proceeding without disruption. Senior officials confirm ample stock to meet farmers' needs for the upcoming season. The government is actively monitoring the situation and has contingency plans in place. Diversified raw material imports are also being secured to ensure continued availability.

items.title: For fair IBC recovery, rethink how it handles instances of insider lending in corporate insolvency cases
items.summary: Related-party creditors of insolvent firms should be asked to show that the credit extended was not actually equity in disguise. As insider loans blur the line between risk capital and debt, their privileged rank in the recovery waterfall needs a rethink.

items.title: Leela Palaces ramps up expansion to tap India’s booming luxury travel
items.summary: Leela Palaces Hotels is accelerating expansion in India's luxury hospitality with nine new properties. With a solid financial position and rising demand, the company aims to have 24 hotels by FY30 while enhancing member loyalty through exclusive clubs.

items.title: Investors to keep a track of West Asia war, crude oil prices in holiday-shortened week: Analysts
items.summary: Developments related to the ongoing month-long war in West Asia, its impact on crude oil prices, and global trends would continue to be key drivers for domestic equities in the holiday-shortened week ahead, analysts said.

items.title: Vedanta's Anil Agarwal invokes Gita in first statement on Jaypee bid reversal: ‘We have no attachment to this asset’
items.summary: Invoking the lines from Gita, Anil Agarwal said Vedanta had no attachment to the asset and talked about fulfilling Dharma. He indicated that Vedanta will challenge the decision in a legal forum.

items.title: WTO talks near deal on reform roadmap amid US-India e-commerce deadlock
items.summary: Global trade ministers are nearing a World Trade Organization reform agreement, with crucial talks underway in Cameroon. A key sticking point remains the extension of a moratorium on customs duties for digital downloads, a move vital for U.S. support. India's stance on a shorter extension is creating tension, potentially impacting the WTO's future relevance amidst ongoing trade disputes.

items.title: PetroChina Profit Drops on Lower Oil Cost, Weak Fuel Demand
items.summary: PetroChina Co.’s earnings fell last year, as softer crude oil prices and sluggish fuel demand weighed on profits.

items.title: India sends 38,000 MT petroleum to Sri Lanka amid global energy crisis
items.summary: India has sent 38,000 metric tons of fuel to Sri Lanka. This shipment arrived on March 28, 2026, after discussions between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Anura Kumara Disanayaka. The fuel includes diesel and petrol. This support comes as Sri Lanka faces supply disruptions due to the Middle East conflict.

items.title: India sends 38,000 MT petroleum to Sri Lanka amid global energy crisis
items.summary: India has shipped 38,000 metric tons of petroleum, including 20,000 MT of diesel and 18,000 MT of petrol, to Sri Lanka. This crucial supply arrived on March 28, following a conversation between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Sri Lankan President Anura Kumara Disanayaka.

items.title: Mcap: 7 of top-10 most valued firms shed ₹1.75 lakh cr; Reliance biggest laggard
items.summary: Last week, the BSE benchmark Sensex lost 949.74 points or 1.27 per cent, and the NSE Nifty tanked 294.9 points or 1.27 per cent.

items.summary: New store in Dallas, US, to open soon as well, says CaratLane Managing Director Saumen Bhaumik

items.title: Mcap of 7 of top-10 most valued firms drops by Rs 1.75 lakh cr; Reliance biggest laggard
items.summary: The combined market valuation of seven of the top-10 most valued firms tumbled by Rs 1.75 lakh crore in a holiday-shortened last week, with Reliance Industries taking the biggest hit, in tandem with a weak trend in equities.

items.title: Finance Ministry flags rising external risks as trade deficit and CAD expand
items.summary: India's economy faces growing external pressures. Trade dynamics are weakening, with imports surging and exports declining. This widens the trade deficit significantly. Capital flows are negative due to global uncertainty. Rising crude oil prices add further strain. These factors are impacting the Indian Rupee.

items.title: NCLAT dismisses BSE pleas challenging NCLT’s power to defreeze demat accounts in insolvency cases
items.summary: NCLAT dismissed BSE’s appeals against NCLT orders to defreeze demat accounts of insolvent firms, ruling that NCLT has jurisdiction under the IBC. The tribunal said dues leading to account freezes qualify as debt, allowing resolution professionals to access shares and proceed with recovery during insolvency proceedings.

items.title: Dr Reddy’s gets 30 days to sell ‘Olymviq’ stock in Novo trademark case
items.summary: Dr Reddy’s has agreed to rebrand semaglutide drug amid a dispute with Novo Nordisk.

items.title: Jio Financial Services expects to begin life, general insurance biz in 2026
items.summary: Jio Financial Services (JFS) is hoping to start general and life insurance businesses this year, a top company official has said.

items.title: Manu Joseph: The paradox of being both upper-caste and dark-skinned in a deeply unequal society
items.summary: Even those atop India’s social hierarchy can be treated poorly for the colour of their skin. This can be a lonely experience. People in traditionally marginalized groups can at least seek comfort in the fellowship of shared marginalization.

items.title: Zerodha issues clarification on doubling brokerage fees for certain F&O trades to Rs 40
items.summary: Zerodha clarified its brokerage fee hike to Rs 40 per order, affecting only a small fraction of intraday derivatives traders using collateral margin with a significant cash shortfall. This move aims to align with SEBI regulations and reduce the broker's capital exposure, as many traders pledge stocks without sufficient cash.

items.title: Rajasthan’s White Wonderland: Marble waste dumping yard becomes scenic hotspot despite toxic risks
items.summary: Kishangarh's vast white marble waste dumping yard draws thousands daily. Once a pollution hotspot, it now attracts tourists for its unique scenery and photo opportunities. Celebrities and pre-wedding shoots boost its popularity. The marble association manages the site, generating revenue for maintenance. While visitors enjoy the landscape, environmentalists warn of health hazards and contamination.

items.title: Global PE firm Advent International eyes 25% stake in potato-maker Iscon Balaji
items.summary: Advent International is in early talks to buy a 25% stake in Iscon Balaji Foods at a  ₹5,000 crore valuation, as investor appetite for India’s frozen potato and QSR supply chain strengthens.

items.title: Mann Ki Baat: Modi credits 10 years of India’s global relations, strengths to help navigate war impact
items.summary: “I once again urge all 140 crore fellow citizens that we must come together as one to overcome this challenge. I appeal to all my fellow citizens to stay vigilant and not fall prey to rumours,” he said

items.summary: The Government has created a strong enabling framework and industries can complement this by ensuring continuity, supporting smaller enterprises and maintaining confidence across the economy

items.title: MP to expand aviation network to 10 airports in two years: Mohan Yadav
items.summary: Madhya Pradesh is set to expand its aviation network, with Chief Minister Mohan Yadav announcing plans for 10 airports within two years as new facilities at Shivpuri and Ujjain become operational. This expansion, alongside the renovation of Indore's Devi Ahilyabai Holkar Airport, aims to significantly boost regional connectivity and passenger handling capacity.

items.summary: On Saturday, the government conducted a thorough review of the hurdles and challenges posed to take up the Metro Rail expansion

items.summary: RBI’s directive on compensation for digital fraud is a whip-crack that banks must heed. They must invest in high-end systems designed explicitly to safeguard customers. As online scams grow more sophisticated, data sharing networks will help.

items.title: CMS Info Systems acquires FSS's ATM managed services business for Rs 115 crore
items.summary: CMS Info Systems has acquired FSS's ATM Managed Services business. This move signals consolidation in India's ATM sector. The deal adds 8,000 ATMs to CMS's network. items.summary: Vedanta is preparing to split into five distinct listed companies next month. This significant restructuring aims to unlock substantial shareholder value. The demerger will allow each new entity greater autonomy for growth. Vedanta's market value is projected to increase considerably following this strategic move. The company's debt will also be managed across the new firms.

items.title: Israel-Iran war: Remittances, rupee at risk; fiscal strain may rise, warns SBI Funds Management report
items.summary: India faces economic challenges due to the West Asia conflict. Remittances from the Gulf may decline, impacting overseas Indians. Higher crude oil prices could widen the current account deficit. The Indian rupee might weaken further. Government finances could be strained by rising subsidy costs, particularly for fertilizers. These factors signal potential pressure on India's external sector and financial markets.

items.title: Consumer panel asks Ola Electric to replace consumer's faulty scooter
items.summary: The commission was of the view that the consistent dropping of the battery level and other issues that arose from the first ride showed the vehicle was defective.

items.summary: BSE had contended that the NCLT does not have any jurisdiction under Section 60 (5) of the IBC Code to adjudicate on the issues, which are within the Securities Law Framework and Sebi Circulars
